title: Using SVG in HTML Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential methods and properties for using vector graphics in your web pages.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of 'SVG' as an image format?
options: It supports millions of colors for photographs.
It is infinitely scalable without losing quality because it describes shapes using math and XML.
It has a fixed resolution that looks good on all devices.
It is always smaller in file size than a JPEG or PNG.

questions.question: Which of these is the most 'powerful' way to add SVG that allows for CSS styling of individual shapes?
options: Using the <svg> tag inline directly in your HTML document.

questions.question: Which CSS property is used specifically to set the interior color of an SVG shape?
Which CSS property is used specifically to set the border color of an SVG path or shape?
How can you improve the accessibility of an inline SVG for screen reader users?
options: Use the <title> and <desc> tags inside the <svg> element.
SVG is naturally accessible and requires no extra tags.
